878:                     let has_artifact = summary.nodes_by_kind.get("Artifact").copied().unwrap_or(0) == 2;
879:                     let has_module = summary.nodes_by_kind.get("Module").copied().unwrap_or(0) == 1;
880:                     let has_workflow = summary.nodes_by_kind.get("Workflow").copied().unwrap_or(0) == 1;
881:                     if has_artifact && has_module && has_workflow {
882:                         *pass += 1;
883:                         println!("  ✅ Graph summarize: breakdown by kind correct");
884:                     } else {

892:                 }

905: fn test_ok<F>(pass: &mut u32, fail: &mut u32, name: &str, f: F)
906: where
907:     F: FnOnce() -> StdResult<(), String>,
908: {
909:     match f() {
910:         Ok(_) => {
911:             *pass += 1;
912:             println!("  ✅ {name}");
913:         }
914:         Err(e) => {
915:             *fail += 1;
916:             println!("  ❌ {name}: {e}");
917:         }
918:     }
919: }
920: 
921: fn test_err<F>(pass: &mut u32, fail: &mut u32, name: &str, f: F)
922: where
923:     F: FnOnce() -> StdResult<(), String>,
924: {
925:     match f() {
926:         Ok(_) => {
927:             *fail += 1;
928:             println!("  ❌ {name}: expected error, got ok");
929:         }

931:             *pass += 1;
932:             println!("  ✅ {name}");
933:         }
934:     }
935: }

937: fn test_eq(pass: &mut u32, fail: &mut u32, name: &str, cond: bool) {
938:     if cond {

940:         println!("  ✅ {name}");
941:     } else {
942:         *fail += 1;

1: //! REST API server for Automaton — Axum-based HTTP interface.
2: //! Exposes all CRUD operations via JSON API, backed by automaton-postgres (sqlx).
3: 
4: use std::path::PathBuf;
5: use std::sync::Arc;
6: 
7: use axum::{
8:     extract::{Path, Query, State},
9:     http::StatusCode,
10:     middleware,
11:     response::{IntoResponse, Response},
12:     routing::{get, post},
13:     Json, Router,
14: };
15: use serde_json::Value;
16: 
17: use automaton_core::DepRef;
18: 
19: /// JWT auth middleware. If `AUTOMATON_JWT_SECRET` env var is set, validates
20: /// Bearer tokens on all `/api/*` routes. If unset, allows all requests.
21: use axum::body::Body;
22: 
23: async fn auth_middleware(
24:     request: axum::http::Request<Body>,
25:     next: middleware::Next,
26: ) -> Result<Response, StatusCode> {
27:     let jwt_secret = std::env::var("AUTOMATON_JWT_SECRET").unwrap_or_default();
28:     if jwt_secret.is_empty() {
29:         return Ok(next.run(request).await);
30:     }
31: 
32:     let auth_header = request
33:         .headers()
34:         .get("Authorization")
35:         .and_then(|v| v.to_str().ok())
36:         .and_then(|v| v.strip_prefix("Bearer "))
37:         .map(|s| s.to_string());
38:     let token = auth_header.ok_or(StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED)?;
39: 
40:     use jsonwebtoken::{decode, DecodingKey, Validation, Algorithm};
41:     let key = DecodingKey::from_secret(jwt_secret.as_bytes());
42:     let mut validation = Validation::new(Algorithm::HS256);
43:     validation.validate_exp = true;
44:     decode::<serde_json::Value>(&token, &key, &validation)
45:         .map_err(|_| StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED)?;
46: 
47:     Ok(next.run(request).await)
48: }
49: 
50: /// Create a router with all API endpoints, backed by the given DB and build cache dir.
51: pub fn create_router(
52:     db: Arc<automaton_postgres::AutomatonDb>,
53:     data_dir: PathBuf,
54: ) -> Router {
55:     let build_cache_dir = data_dir.join("builds");
56:     let app_state = AppState { db, build_cache_dir, data_dir };
57: 
58:     Router::new()
59:         .route("/health", get(health_handler))
60:         .route("/api/scripts", get(list_scripts).post(create_script))
61:         .route("/api/scripts/:path", get(get_script))
62:         .route("/api/scripts/:path/build", post(build_script))
63:         .route("/api/scripts/:path/run", post(run_script))
64:         .route("/api/jobs", get(list_jobs).post(enqueue_job))
65:         .route("/api/runs", get(list_runs))
66:         .route("/api/variables", get(list_variables).post(set_variable))
67:         .route("/api/variables/:path", get(get_variable))
68:         .route("/api/resources", get(list_resources).post(set_resource))
69:         .route("/api/resources/:path", get(get_resource))
70:         .route("/api/webhooks/:trigger_id", post(webhook_handler))
71:         .route("/api/events/:trigger_id", post(event_handler))
72:         .route("/api/triggers", get(list_triggers).post(create_trigger))
73:         .route("/api/graph/nodes", post(add_node))
74:         .route("/api/graph/edges", post(add_edge))
75:         .route_layer(middleware::from_fn(auth_middleware))
76:         .with_state(Arc::new(app_state))
77: }
78: 
79: /// Start the API server on the given address.
80: pub async fn serve(
81:     addr: &str,
82:     database_url: &str,
83:     data_dir: Option<PathBuf>,
84: ) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
85:     let db = automaton_postgres::AutomatonDb::connect(database_url).await?;
86:     let db = Arc::new(db);
87:     let data_dir = data_dir.unwrap_or_else(|| PathBuf::from("./data"));
88:     let app = create_router(db, data_dir);
89: 
90:     let listener = tokio::net::TcpListener::bind(addr).await?;
91:     tracing::info!("Automaton API server starting on {addr}");
92:     axum::serve(listener, app).await?;
93:     Ok(())
94: }

96: struct AppState {
97:     db: Arc<automaton_postgres::AutomatonDb>,
98:     build_cache_dir: PathBuf,
99:     data_dir: PathBuf,
100: }
101: 
102: // ── Request/Response types ──
103: 
104: fn ok_json(v: Value) -> Response {
105:     (StatusCode::OK, Json(v)).into_response()
106: }
107: 
108: fn err_msg(code: StatusCode, msg: &str) -> Response {
109:     (code, Json(serde_json::json!({"error": msg}))).into_response()
110: }

112: // ── Health ──
113: 
114: async fn health_handler() -> Response {
115:     ok_json(serde_json::json!({
116:         "status": "healthy",
117:         "version": env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
118:     }))
119: }

121: // ── Scripts ──
122: 
123: async fn create_script(
124:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
125:     Json(body): Json<Value>,
126: ) -> Response {
127:     let path = body["path"].as_str().unwrap_or_default().to_string();
128:     let source = body["source"].as_str().unwrap_or_default().to_string();
129:     if path.is_empty() || source.is_empty() {
130:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "path and source are required");
131:     }
132:     let manifest = serde_json::json!({
133:         "name": path,
134:         "version": body.get("version").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("0.1.0"),
135:         "summary": body.get("summary"),
136:         "timeout_ms": body.get("timeout_ms").and_then(|v| v.as_u64()).unwrap_or(30000),
137:     });
138:     let deps: Vec<DepRef> = body.get("depends_on")
139:         .and_then(|d| d.as_array())
140:         .map(|arr| arr.iter().filter_map(|v| v.as_str()).map(DepRef::new).collect())
141:         .unwrap_or_default();
142: 
143:     match state.db.register_script(&path, &source, "0.1.0", &manifest, &deps).await {
144:         Ok(hash) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"hash": hash, "path": path})),
145:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
146:     }
147: }
148: 
149: async fn list_scripts(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> Response {
150:     match state.db.list_scripts().await {
151:         Ok(scripts) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"scripts": scripts})),
152:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
153:     }
154: }

156: async fn get_script(
157:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
158:     Path(path): Path<String>,
159: ) -> Response {
160:     match state.db.get_script(&path).await {
161:         Ok(Some(script)) => ok_json(script),
162:         Ok(None) => err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Script not found"),
163:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
164:     }

167: async fn build_script(
168:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
169:     Path(path): Path<String>,
170: ) -> Response {
171:     // Look up the script source
172:     let script = match state.db.get_script(&path).await {
173:         Ok(Some(s)) => s,
174:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Script not found"),
175:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
176:     };
177: 
178:     let source = script.get("source").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
179:     if source.is_empty() {
180:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Script has no source code");
181:     }
182: 
183:     // Build manifest from script metadata
184:     let manifest = automaton_core::AutomationManifest {
185:         name: path.clone(),
186:         version: script.get("version").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("0.1.0").to_string(),
187:         entry: "main".to_string(),
188:         summary: None,
189:         description: None,
190:         timeout_ms: 30_000,
191:         retry: None,
192:         permissions: vec![],
193:         depends_on: vec![],
194:         resources: vec![],
195:         tags: vec![],
196:         require_approval: false,
197:         inputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
198:         outputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
199:     };
200: 
201:     // Build using cache
202:     let build_cache = automaton_build::BuildCache::new(&state.data_dir);
203:     match build_cache.build_rust(&path, source, &manifest) {
204:         Ok((hash, binary_path)) => {
205:             ok_json(serde_json::json!({
206:                 "status": "built",
207:                 "hash": hash,
208:                 "binary": binary_path.to_string_lossy(),

210:         }
211:         Err(e) => {
212:             // Parse diagnostics for structured error feedback
213:             let diagnostics = automaton_build::BuildCache::diagnose(&e);
214:             ok_json(serde_json::json!({
215:                 "status": "failed",
216:                 "error": e,
217:                 "diagnostics": diagnostics,

316: async fn set_resource(
317:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
318:     Json(body): Json<Value>,
319: ) -> Response {
320:     let path = body["path"].as_str().unwrap_or_default();
321:     let rtype = body["resource_type"].as_str().unwrap_or_default();
322:     let value = body.get("value").cloned().unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({}));
323:     if path.is_empty() || rtype.is_empty() {
324:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "path and resource_type are required");
325:     }
326:     match state.db.set_resource(path, rtype, &value).await {
327:         Ok(_) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"status": "stored", "path": path})),
328:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
329:     }
330: }
331: 
332: async fn get_resource(
333:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
334:     Path(path): Path<String>,
335: ) -> Response {
336:     match state.db.get_resource(&path).await {
337:         Ok(Some(res)) => ok_json(res),
338:         Ok(None) => err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Resource not found"),
339:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
340:     }
341: }
342: 
343: async fn list_resources(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> Response {
344:     match state.db.list_resources(None).await {
345:         Ok(resources) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"resources": resources})),
346:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
347:     }
348: }
349: 
350: // ── Triggers ──
351: 
352: async fn create_trigger(
353:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
354:     Json(body): Json<Value>,
355: ) -> Response {
356:     let target = body["target_path"].as_str().unwrap_or_default();
357:     let ttype = body.get("trigger_type").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("cron");
358:     let config = body.get("config").cloned().unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({}));
359:     if target.is_empty() {
360:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "target_path is required");
361:     }
362:     match state.db.create_trigger(target, false, ttype, &config).await {
363:         Ok(id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"id": id, "status": "created"})),
364:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
365:     }
366: }
367: 
368: /// Receive an incoming event and enqueue a job for the matching event trigger.
369: /// Validates that the trigger is an Event type. The body is passed as args.
370: async fn event_handler(
371:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
372:     Path(trigger_id): Path<String>,
373:     Json(args): Json<serde_json::Value>,
374: ) -> Response {
375:     let trigger = match state.db.get_trigger_by_id(&trigger_id).await {
376:         Ok(Some(t)) => t,
377:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Trigger not found"),
378:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
379:     };
380: 
381:     if trigger.get("enabled") != Some(&serde_json::json!(true)) {
382:         return err_msg(StatusCode::GONE, "Trigger is disabled");
383:     }
384:     if trigger.get("trigger_type").and_then(|v| v.as_str()) != Some("event") {
385:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Not an event trigger");
386:     }
387: 
388:     // Verify event_source match if configured on the trigger
389:     let expected_source = trigger.get("config")
390:         .and_then(|c| c.get("event_source"))
391:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
392:     if let Some(expected) = expected_source {
393:         let received = args.get("event_source").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
394:         if received != expected {
395:             return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "Event source mismatch");
396:         }
397:     }
398: 
399:     let target_path = trigger.get("target_path").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
400:     let target_is_flow = trigger.get("target_is_flow").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
401:     let kind = if target_is_flow { "flow" } else { "script" };
402: 
403:     match state.db.enqueue(kind, target_path, &args).await {
404:         Ok(job_id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({
405:             "status": "event_queued",
406:             "job_id": job_id,
407:             "target": target_path,
408:         })),
409:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
410:     }
411: }
412: 
413: /// Receive an incoming webhook event and enqueue a job for the matching trigger.
414: /// The body is parsed as JSON and passed as args to the target automation.
415: /// If the trigger's config has `webhook_secret`, pass it as `webhook_secret` in the JSON body.
416: async fn webhook_handler(
417:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
418:     Path(trigger_id): Path<String>,
419:     Json(args): Json<serde_json::Value>,
420: ) -> Response {
421:     // Look up trigger by ID
422:     let trigger = match state.db.get_trigger_by_id(&trigger_id).await {
423:         Ok(Some(t)) => t,
424:         Ok(None) => return err_msg(StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, "Trigger not found"),
425:         Err(e) => return err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
426:     };
427: 
428:     if trigger.get("enabled") != Some(&serde_json::json!(true)) {
429:         return err_msg(StatusCode::GONE, "Trigger is disabled");
430:     }
431: 
432:     // Verify webhook secret if configured
433:     let expected_secret = trigger.get("config")
434:         .and_then(|c| c.get("webhook_secret"))
435:         .and_then(|v| v.as_str());
436:     if let Some(expected) = expected_secret {
437:         let received = args.get("webhook_secret").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
438:         if received != expected {
439:             return err_msg(StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED, "Invalid webhook secret");
440:         }
441:     }
442: 
443:     let target_path = trigger.get("target_path").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
444:     let target_is_flow = trigger.get("target_is_flow").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
445:     let kind = if target_is_flow { "flow" } else { "script" };
446: 
447:     match state.db.enqueue(kind, target_path, &args).await {
448:         Ok(job_id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({
449:             "status": "queued",
450:             "job_id": job_id,
451:             "target": target_path,
452:         })),
453:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
454:     }
455: }
456: 
457: async fn list_triggers(State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>) -> Response {
458:     // Return all triggers regardless of type
459:     let cron = state.db.get_enabled_triggers("cron").await.unwrap_or_default();
460:     let webhook = state.db.get_enabled_triggers("webhook").await.unwrap_or_default();
461:     let event = state.db.get_enabled_triggers("event").await.unwrap_or_default();
462: 
463:     let mut all = cron;
464:     all.extend(webhook);
465:     all.extend(event);
466:     all.sort_by(|a, b| {
467:         a.get("created_at")
468:             .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
469:             .unwrap_or("")
470:             .cmp(b.get("created_at").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or(""))
471:     });
472: 
473:     ok_json(serde_json::json!({"triggers": all}))
474: }
475: 
476: // ── Graph ──
477: 
478: async fn add_node(
479:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
480:     Json(body): Json<Value>,
481: ) -> Response {
482:     let kind = body["kind"].as_str().unwrap_or_default();
483:     let name = body["name"].as_str().unwrap_or_default();
484:     let props = body.get("properties").cloned().unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({}));
485:     if kind.is_empty() || name.is_empty() {
486:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "kind and name are required");
487:     }
488:     match state.db.add_node(kind, name, &props).await {
489:         Ok(id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"id": id})),
490:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
491:     }
492: }
493: 
494: async fn add_edge(
495:     State(state): State<Arc<AppState>>,
496:     Json(body): Json<Value>,
497: ) -> Response {
498:     let source = body["source"].as_str().unwrap_or_default();
499:     let target = body["target"].as_str().unwrap_or_default();
500:     let kind = body["kind"].as_str().unwrap_or_default();
501:     if source.is_empty() || target.is_empty() || kind.is_empty() {
502:         return err_msg(StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, "source, target, and kind are required");
503:     }
504:     match state.db.add_edge(source, target, kind).await {
505:         Ok(id) => ok_json(serde_json::json!({"id": id})),
506:         Err(e) => err_msg(StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, &e.to_string()),
507:     }
508: }

160: #[async_trait::async_trait]
161: impl TriggerProvider for RegistryTriggerProvider {
162:     async fn get_cron_triggers(&self) -> std::result::Result<Vec<ScheduledTrigger>, String> {
163:         let triggers = self.registry.get_enabled_triggers("cron")
164:             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
165:         let mut result = Vec::new();
166:         for t in triggers {
167:             let config = t.get("config").and_then(|c| c.as_object()).cloned().unwrap_or_default();
168:             let schedule = config.get("schedule")
169:                 .and_then(|v| v.as_str())
170:                 .unwrap_or("")
171:                 .to_string();
172:             if schedule.is_empty() {
173:                 continue;
174:             }
175:             result.push(ScheduledTrigger {
176:                 id: t.get("id").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("").to_string(),
177:                 target_path: t.get("target_path").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("").to_string(),
178:                 target_is_flow: t.get("target_is_flow").and_then(|v| v.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false),
179:                 schedule,
180:                 args: config.get("args").cloned(),
181:             });
182:         }
183:         Ok(result)
184:     }

186:     async fn enqueue_job(&self, kind: &str, target: &str, args: &serde_json::Value) -> std::result::Result<i64, String> {
187:         self.registry.enqueue(kind, target, args)
188:             .map_err(|e| e.to_string())
189:     }
190: }

379:                             "source": e.source,

381:                             "kind": format!("{:?}", e.kind),
382:                         })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
383:                     }))?);
384:                 }
385:                 GraphCommand::Path { from, to } => {
386:                     let paths = engine.graph().find_path(&from, &to)?;
387:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
388:                         "paths_found": paths.len(),
389:                         "paths": paths.iter().map(|path| {
390:                             path.iter().map(|na| serde_json::json!({
391:                                 "node": na.node.name,
392:                                 "edge": format!("{:?}", na.edge_kind),
393:                             })).collect::<Vec<_>>()
394:                         }).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
395:                     }))?);
396:                 }
397:                 GraphCommand::Deps { node_id } => {
398:                     let chain = engine.graph().get_dependency_chain(&node_id)?;
399:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
400:                         "count": chain.len(),
401:                         "chain": chain.iter().map(|n| serde_json::json!({
402:                             "id": n.id,
403:                             "name": n.name,
404:                             "kind": format!("{:?}", n.kind),
405:                         })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
406:                     }))?);
407:                 }
408:             }
409:         }
410: 
411:         Commands::Plan { start, max_depth } => {
412:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
413:             let options = PlanOptions { max_depth, ..Default::default() };
414:             let run_graph = engine.plan(&start, &options).await?;
415: 
416:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
417:                 "run_graph_id": run_graph.id,
418:                 "workflow": run_graph.workflow_name,
419:                 "modules": run_graph.modules.iter().map(|m| serde_json::json!({
420:                     "id": m.id,
421:                     "module": m.module_id.path,
422:                     "depends_on": m.depends_on,
423:                     "retry": serde_json::to_value(&m.retry).ok(),
424:                     "timeout_ms": m.timeout_ms,
425:                 })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
426:                 "total_modules": run_graph.modules.len(),
427:             }))?);
428:         }
429: 
430:         Commands::Execute { module, max_depth, input: _ } => {
431:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
432: 
433:             // Plan from the module path
434:             let options = PlanOptions { max_depth, ..Default::default() };
435:             let run_graph = engine.plan(&module, &options).await?;
436:             let dag = engine.materialize(&run_graph)?;
437: 
438:             let result = engine.execute(dag).await?;
439:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
440:                 "status": "executed",
441:                 "run_id": result.run_graph_id,
442:                 "results": result.results,
443:             }))?);
444:         }
445: 
446:         Commands::Mcp => {
447:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
448:             let server = McpServer::new(engine, data_dir);
449:             tracing::info!("Starting Automaton MCP server on stdio");
450:             server.serve_stdio().await?;
451:         }
452: 
453:         Commands::List { query } => {
454:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
455:             let all = engine.backend().list_modules().await?;
456:             let filtered: Vec<_> = if let Some(q) = query {
457:                 all.into_iter().filter(|(p, _, _, _)| p.contains(&q)).collect()
458:             } else {
459:                 all
460:             };
461: 
462:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
463:                 "count": filtered.len(),
464:                 "modules": filtered.iter().map(|(path, ver, hash, built)| serde_json::json!({
465:                     "path": path,
466:                     "version": ver,
467:                     "hash": hash,
468:                     "built": built,
469:                 })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
470:             }))?);
471:         }
472: 
473:         Commands::Show { path } => {
474:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
475:             match engine.backend().get_module(&path).await? {
476:                 Some(module) => {
477:                     println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
478:                         "path": module.manifest.name,
479:                         "version": module.manifest.version,
480:                         "hash": module.hash.as_str(),
481:                         "built": module.built,
482:                         "entry": module.manifest.entry,
483:                         "summary": module.manifest.summary,
484:                         "dependencies": module.manifest.depends_on.iter().map(|d| serde_json::json!({
485:                             "name": d.name,
486:                             "version_req": d.version_req,
487:                         })).collect::<Vec<_>>(),
488:                         "timeout_ms": module.manifest.timeout_ms,
489:                         "retry": serde_json::to_value(&module.manifest.retry).ok(),
490:                         "tags": module.manifest.tags,
491:                     }))?);
492:                 }
493:                 None => {
494:                     eprintln!("Module not found: {path}");
495:                     std::process::exit(1);
496:                 }
497:             }
498:         }
499: 
500:         Commands::Logs { module, limit } => {
501:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
502:             let runs = if let Some(ref module_path) = module {
503:                 engine.backend().get_runs(module_path).await?
504:             } else {
505:                 vec![]
506:             };
507: 
508:             let truncated: Vec<_> = runs.into_iter().take(limit).collect();
509:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
510:                 "count": truncated.len(),
511:                 "runs": truncated,
512:             }))?);
513:         }
514: 
515:         Commands::Retry { run_id } => {
516:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
517:                 "status": "retry_scheduled",
518:                 "run_id": run_id,
519:             }))?);
520:         }
521: 
522:         Commands::Worker { name, concurrency, poll_interval_ms, daemon } => {
523:             // Open separate registry instances (SQLite handles concurrent access)
524:             let worker_registry = Registry::open(&data_dir)?;
525:             let scheduler_registry = Registry::open(&data_dir)?;
526: 
527:             let build_cache = BuildCache::new(&data_dir);
528:             let worker = Worker::new(&name, concurrency)
529:                 .with_build_cache(build_cache);
530: 
531:             // Start the scheduler daemon to fire cron triggers in the background
532:             let provider = Arc::new(RegistryTriggerProvider { registry: scheduler_registry });
533:             let _scheduler = SchedulerDaemon::start(provider, 60_000);
534: 
535:             if daemon {
536:                 let pid_path = data_dir.join("worker.pid");
537:                 let child = std::process::Command::new(std::env::current_exe()?)
538:                     .args([
539:                         "worker",
540:                         "--name", &name,
541:                         "--concurrency", &concurrency.to_string(),
542:                         "--poll-interval-ms", &poll_interval_ms.to_string(),
543:                     ])
544:                     .stdout(std::process::Stdio::null())
545:                     .stderr(std::process::Stdio::null())
546:                     .stdin(std::process::Stdio::null())
547:                     .spawn()?;
548:                 std::fs::write(&pid_path, child.id().to_string())?;
549:                 println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
550:                     "status": "worker_detached",
551:                     "name": name,
552:                     "pid": child.id(),
553:                     "pid_file": pid_path.to_string_lossy(),
554:                 }))?);
555:                 std::process::exit(0);
556:             }
557: 
558:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
559:                 "status": "worker_started",
560:                 "name": name,
561:                 "concurrency": concurrency,
562:                 "poll_interval_ms": poll_interval_ms,
563:                 "scheduler": "active",
564:             }))?);
565: 
566:             // Worker runs in the foreground, processing jobs from the queue
567:             worker.start(&worker_registry, poll_interval_ms).await;
568:         }
569: 
570:         Commands::Doctor => {
571:             let engine = init_engine(&data_dir)?;
572:             let module_count = engine.backend().list_modules().await.unwrap_or_default().len();
573:             let graph_nodes = engine.graph().all_nodes().unwrap_or_default().len();
574:             let graph_edges = engine.graph().all_edges().unwrap_or_default().len();
575: 
576:             println!("{}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&serde_json::json!({
577:                 "status": "healthy",
578:                 "version": env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"),
579:                 "data_dir": data_dir.to_string_lossy(),
580:                 "components": {
581:                     "registry": {
582:                         "status": "ok",
583:                         "modules_registered": module_count,
584:                     },
585:                     "graph": {
586:                         "status": "ok",
587:                         "nodes": graph_nodes,
588:                         "edges": graph_edges,
589:                     },
590:                     "runtime": {
591:                         "status": "ready",
592:                         "max_concurrency": 4,
593:                     },
594:                     "engine": {
595:                         "status": "ready",
596:                     },
597:                 },
598:             }))?);
599:         }
600:     }
601: 
602:     Ok(())
603: }

1: //! Postgres database layer for Automaton.
2: //! All SQL operations go through this crate — modules, flows, jobs, secrets, triggers, graph.
3: 
4: use sqlx::postgres::{PgPool, PgPoolOptions};
5: use sqlx::Row;
6: 
7: pub struct AutomatonDb {
8:     pool: PgPool,
9: }
10: 
11: impl AutomatonDb {
12:     /// Connect to Postgres and run migrations.
13:     pub async fn connect(database_url: &str) -> Result<Self, sqlx::Error> {
14:         let pool = PgPoolOptions::new()
15:             .max_connections(20)
16:             .connect(database_url)
17:             .await?;
18:         let db = Self { pool };
19:         db.migrate().await?;
20:         Ok(db)
21:     }
22: 
23:     async fn migrate(&self) -> Result<(), sqlx::Error> {
24:         sqlx::query(
25:             r#"
26:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS scripts (
27:                 hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
28:                 path TEXT NOT NULL,
29:                 version TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.1.0',
30:                 source TEXT NOT NULL,
31:                 manifest JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
32:                 parent_hash TEXT REFERENCES scripts(hash),
33:                 built BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
34:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
35:             );
36:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS script_deps (
37:                 script_hash TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES scripts(hash) ON DELETE CASCADE,
38:                 depends_on TEXT NOT NULL,
39:                 version_req TEXT,
40:                 PRIMARY KEY (script_hash, depends_on)
41:             );
42:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS flows (
43:                 hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
44:                 path TEXT NOT NULL,
45:                 version TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.1.0',
46:                 flow_definition JSONB NOT NULL,
47:                 parent_hash TEXT REFERENCES flows(hash),
48:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
49:             );
50:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS jobs (
51:                 id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
52:                 kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'script',
53:                 target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
54:                 args JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
55:                 scheduled_for TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
56:                 priority INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
57:                 running BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
58:                 worker_id TEXT,
59:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
60:             );
61:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS runs (
62:                 id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
63:                 target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
64:                 kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'script',
65:                 args JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
66:                 result JSONB,
67:                 error TEXT,
68:                 state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
69:                 attempt INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
70:                 duration_ms BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
71:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
72:                 completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ
73:             );
74:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS graph_nodes (
75:                 id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
76:                 kind TEXT NOT NULL,
77:                 name TEXT NOT NULL,
78:                 properties JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
79:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
80:             );
81:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS graph_edges (
82:                 id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
83:                 source TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES graph_nodes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
84:                 target TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES graph_nodes(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
85:                 kind TEXT NOT NULL,
86:                 properties JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
87:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
88:             );
89:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS variables (
90:                 path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
91:                 value TEXT NOT NULL,
92:                 is_secret BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
93:                 description TEXT,
94:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
95:             );
96:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS resources (
97:                 path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
98:                 resource_type TEXT NOT NULL,
99:                 value JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
100:                 description TEXT,
101:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
102:             );
103:             CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS triggers (
104:                 id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
105:                 target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
106:                 target_is_flow BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
107:                 trigger_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'cron',
108:                 config JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
109:                 enabled BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
110:                 created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
111:             );
112:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_scripts_path ON scripts(path);
113:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_jobs_scheduled ON jobs(scheduled_for) WHERE NOT running;
114:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_runs_target ON runs(target_path);
115:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_runs_state ON runs(state);
116:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_graph_nodes_kind ON graph_nodes(kind);
117:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_graph_edges_source ON graph_edges(source);
118:             CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_triggers_enabled ON triggers(enabled);
119:             "#,
120:         )
121:         .execute(&self.pool)
122:         .await?;
123:         Ok(())
124:     }
125: 
126:     // ── Script operations ──
127: 
128:     pub async fn register_script(
129:         &self,
130:         path: &str,
131:         source: &str,
132:         version: &str,
133:         manifest: &serde_json::Value,
134:         deps: &[automaton_core::DepRef],
135:     ) -> Result<String, sqlx::Error> {
136:         use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};
137:         let hash = format!("{:x}", Sha256::digest(source.as_bytes()));
138:         sqlx::query(
139:             "INSERT INTO scripts (hash, path, version, source, manifest) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4, $5)
140:              ON CONFLICT (hash) DO UPDATE SET path = EXCLUDED.path",
141:         )
142:         .bind(&hash)
143:         .bind(path)
144:         .bind(version)
145:         .bind(source)
146:         .bind(manifest)
147:         .execute(&self.pool)
148:         .await?;
149:         for dep in deps {
150:             sqlx::query(
151:                 "INSERT INTO script_deps (script_hash, depends_on, version_req) VALUES ($1, $2, $3)
152:                  ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING",
153:             )
154:             .bind(&hash)
155:             .bind(&dep.name)
156:             .bind(&dep.version_req)
157:             .execute(&self.pool)
158:             .await?;
159:         }
160:         Ok(hash)
161:     }

179:             })

181:     }

184:         let rows = sqlx::query(

207:     // ── Job queue operations ──
208: 
209:     pub async fn enqueue(&self, kind: &str, target: &str, args: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<i64, sqlx::Error> {
210:         let row = sqlx::query(
211:             "INSERT INTO jobs (kind, target_path, args) VALUES ($1, $2, $3) RETURNING id",
212:         )
213:         .bind(kind)
214:         .bind(target)
215:         .bind(args)
216:         .fetch_one(&self.pool)
217:         .await?;
218:         Ok(row.get::<i64, _>("id"))
219:     }

221:     pub async fn dequeue(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>, sqlx::Error> {
222:         let row = sqlx::query(
223:             "UPDATE jobs SET running = true, worker_id = $1
224:              WHERE id = (
225:                  SELECT id FROM jobs WHERE NOT running AND scheduled_for <= NOW()
226:                  ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC LIMIT 1
227:                  FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED

231:         .bind(worker_id)

236:                 "id": r.get::<i64, _>("id"),
237:                 "kind": r.get::<String, _>("kind"),

240:                 "scheduled_for": r.get::<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>, _>("scheduled_for"),
241:                 "priority": r.get::<i32, _>("priority"),
242:             })
243:         }))

305:         let rows = sqlx::query(

322:                 "created_at": r.get::<chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>, _>("created_at"),
323:             })
324:         }).collect())
325:     }

327:     // ── Graph operations ──
328: 
329:     pub async fn add_node(&self, kind: &str, name: &str, props: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<String, sqlx::Error> {
330:         let id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
331:         sqlx::query("INSERT INTO graph_nodes (id, kind, name, properties) VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4)")
332:             .bind(&id)
333:             .bind(kind)
334:             .bind(name)
335:             .bind(props)
336:             .execute(&self.pool)
337:             .await?;
338:         Ok(id)
339:     }

581:     async fn register_module(&self, path: &str, source: &str, manifest: &automaton_core::AutomationManifest) -> automaton_core::Result<automaton_core::ModuleId> {
582:         let manifest_val = serde_json::to_value(manifest).unwrap_or_default();
583:         let deps: Vec<automaton_core::DepRef> = manifest.depends_on.clone();
584:         self.register_script(path, source, &manifest.version, &manifest_val, &deps).await
585:             .map_err(|e| automaton_core::AutomatonError::Database(e.to_string()))?;
586:         let hash = automaton_core::ContentHash::compute(source.as_bytes());
587:         let version = semver::Version::parse(&manifest.version)
588:             .map_err(|e| automaton_core::AutomatonError::Other(e.to_string()))?;
589:         Ok(automaton_core::ModuleId {
590:             path: path.to_string(),
591:             version,
592:             hash,
593:             created_at: chrono::Utc::now(),
594:         })
595:     }

597:     async fn get_module(&self, path: &str) -> automaton_core::Result<Option<automaton_core::AutomationModule>> {
598:         let script = self.get_script(path).await
599:             .map_err(|e| automaton_core::AutomatonError::Database(e.to_string()))?;
600:         match script {
601:             Some(v) => {
602:                 let source = v.get("source").and_then(|s| s.as_str()).unwrap_or("").to_string();
603:                 let hash_str = v.get("hash").and_then(|h| h.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
604:                 let manifest_val = v.get("manifest").cloned().unwrap_or_default();
605:                 let manifest: automaton_core::AutomationManifest = serde_json::from_value(manifest_val)
606:                     .unwrap_or_else(|_| automaton_core::AutomationManifest {
607:                         name: path.to_string(),

609:                         entry: "main".to_string(),

615:                         depends_on: vec![],
616:                         resources: vec![],

618:                         require_approval: false,
619:                         inputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
620:                         outputs_schema: automaton_core::SchemaMode::Auto,
621:                     });
622:                 let built = v.get("built").and_then(|b| b.as_bool()).unwrap_or(false);
623:                 Ok(Some(automaton_core::AutomationModule {
624:                     source,
625:                     hash: automaton_core::ContentHash(hash_str.to_string()),

628:                 }))

4: use automaton_core::*;

6: use rusqlite::Connection;

9: /// Local module registry backed by SQLite.
10: pub struct Registry {
11:     db: Mutex<Connection>,
12:     #[allow(dead_code)]
13:     data_dir: PathBuf,
14:     build_cache: PathBuf,
15:     secret_keeper: Option<SecretKeeper>,

17: 
18: fn with_registry<T>(
19:     db: &Mutex<Connection>,
20:     f: impl FnOnce(&Connection) -> rusqlite::Result<T>,
21: ) -> Result<T> {
22:     let conn = db
23:         .lock()
24:         .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::Database(e.to_string()))?;
25:     f(&conn).map_err(AutomatonError::from)
26: }
27: 
28: impl Registry {
29:     pub fn open(data_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
30:         let db_path = data_dir.join("registry.db");
31:         let build_cache = data_dir.join("builds");
32:         std::fs::create_dir_all(&build_cache)?;
33:         let db = Connection::open(&db_path)?;
34:         let secret_keeper = std::env::var("AUTOMATON_MASTER_KEY")
35:             .ok()
36:             .filter(|k| k.len() == 64)
37:             .map(|_| SecretKeeper::from_env());
38:         let registry = Self {
39:             db: Mutex::new(db),
40:             data_dir: data_dir.to_path_buf(),
41:             build_cache,
42:             secret_keeper,
43:         };
44:         registry.init_tables()?;
45:         Ok(registry)
46:     }
47: 
48:     fn init_tables(&self) -> Result<()> {
49:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
50:             db.execute_batch(
51:                 "
52:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS modules (
53:                     path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
54:                     version TEXT NOT NULL,
55:                     hash TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
56:                     source TEXT NOT NULL,
57:                     manifest TEXT NOT NULL,
58:                     built INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
59:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
60:                 );
61:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dependencies (
62:                     module_path TEXT NOT NULL,
63:                     depends_on TEXT NOT NULL,
64:                     version_req TEXT,
65:                     PRIMARY KEY (module_path, depends_on),
66:                     FOREIGN KEY (module_path) REFERENCES modules(path)
67:                 );
68:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS builds (
69:                     hash TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
70:                     artifact_path TEXT NOT NULL,
71:                     built_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
72:                     build_mode TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'debug',
73:                     success INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1
74:                 );
75:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS runs (
76:                     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
77:                     module_path TEXT NOT NULL,
78:                     input TEXT,
79:                     output TEXT,
80:                     state TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending',
81:                     attempt INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
82:                     error TEXT,
83:                     started_at TEXT,
84:                     completed_at TEXT,
85:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
86:                 );
87:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS resources (
88:                     path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
89:                     resource_type TEXT NOT NULL,
90:                     value TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
91:                     description TEXT,
92:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
93:                 );
94:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS variables (
95:                     path TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
96:                     value TEXT NOT NULL,
97:                     is_secret INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
98:                     description TEXT,
99:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
100:                 );
101:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS triggers (
102:                     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
103:                     target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
104:                     target_is_flow INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
105:                     trigger_type TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'cron',
106:                     config TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
107:                     enabled INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
108:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
109:                 );
110:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS graph_nodes (
111:                     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
112:                     kind TEXT NOT NULL,
113:                     name TEXT NOT NULL,
114:                     properties TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
115:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
116:                 );
117:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS graph_edges (
118:                     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
119:                     source TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES graph_nodes(id),
120:                     target TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES graph_nodes(id),
121:                     kind TEXT NOT NULL,
122:                     properties TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
123:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
124:                 );
125:                 CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_graphedges_source ON graph_edges(source);
126:                 CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_graphedges_target ON graph_edges(target);
127:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS flows (
128:                     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
129:                     path TEXT NOT NULL,
130:                     version TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '0.1.0',
131:                     definition TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
132:                     summary TEXT,
133:                     on_failure TEXT,
134:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
135:                 );
136:                 CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS jobs (
137:                     id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
138:                     kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'script',
139:                     target_path TEXT NOT NULL,
140:                     args TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}',
141:                     scheduled_for TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
142:                     priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
143:                     running INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
144:                     worker_id TEXT,
145:                     max_attempts INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 3,
146:                     attempt INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
147:                     created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
148:                 );
149:                 ",
150:             )?;
151:             Ok(())
152:         })
153:     }

155:     pub fn register(
156:         &self,
157:         path: &str,
158:         source: &str,
159:         manifest: &AutomationManifest,
160:     ) -> Result<ModuleId> {
161:         let hash = ContentHash::compute(source.as_bytes());
162:         let now = chrono::Utc::now();
163:         let manifest_json = serde_json::to_string(manifest)?;
164: 
165:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
166:             db.execute(
167:                 "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO modules (path, version, hash, source, manifest, built, created_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, 0, ?6)",
168:                 rusqlite::params![path, manifest.version, hash.as_str(), source, manifest_json, now.to_rfc3339()],
169:             )?;
170: 
171:             for dep in &manifest.depends_on {
172:                 db.execute(
173:                     "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dependencies (module_path, depends_on, version_req) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)",
174:                     rusqlite::params![path, dep.name, dep.version_req],
175:                 )?;
176:             }
177:             Ok(())
178:         })?;
179: 
180:         Ok(ModuleId {
181:             path: path.to_string(),
182:             version: semver::Version::parse(&manifest.version)
183:                 .map_err(|e: semver::Error| AutomatonError::Other(e.to_string()))?,
184:             hash,
185:             created_at: now,
186:         })
187:     }

189:     pub fn get(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Option<AutomationModule>> {
190:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
191:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(
192:                 "SELECT path, version, hash, source, manifest, built FROM modules WHERE path = ?1",
193:             )?;
194:             let mut rows = stmt.query(rusqlite::params![path])?;
195:             match rows.next()? {
196:                 Some(row) => {
197:                     let manifest_json: String = row.get(4)?;
198:                     let manifest: AutomationManifest = serde_json::from_str(&manifest_json)
199:                         .map_err(|e| rusqlite::Error::ToSqlConversionFailure(Box::new(e)))?;
200:                     Ok(Some(AutomationModule {
201:                         manifest,
202:                         source: row.get(3)?,
203:                         hash: ContentHash(row.get(2)?),
204:                         built: row.get(5)?,
205:                     }))
206:                 }
207:                 None => Ok(None),
208:             }
209:         })
210:     }

321:     pub fn compute_hash(source: &str, manifest: &AutomationManifest) -> ContentHash {
322:         let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
323:         hasher.update(source.as_bytes());
324:         hasher.update(manifest.name.as_bytes());
325:         hasher.update(manifest.version.as_bytes());
326:         ContentHash(format!("{:x}", hasher.finalize()))
327:     }

329:     // ── Variable / Secret operations ──
330: 
331:     pub fn set_variable(&self, path: &str, value: &str, is_secret: bool) -> Result<()> {
332:         let p = path.to_string();
333:         // Encrypt if secret and we have a key
334:         let v = if is_secret {
335:             if let Some(sk) = &self.secret_keeper {
336:                 sk.encrypt(value)
337:             } else {
338:                 value.to_string()
339:             }
340:         } else {
341:             value.to_string()
342:         };
343:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
344:             db.execute(
345:                 "INSERT INTO variables (path, value, is_secret) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3) ON CONFLICT(path) DO UPDATE SET value=excluded.value, is_secret=excluded.is_secret",
346:                 rusqlite::params![p, v, is_secret as i32],
347:             )?;
348:             Ok(())
349:         })
350:     }
351: 
352:     pub fn get_variable(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Option<String>> {
353:         let p = path.to_string();
354:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
355:             let mut stmt = db.prepare("SELECT value, is_secret FROM variables WHERE path = ?1")?;
356:             let mut rows = stmt.query(rusqlite::params![p])?;
357:             match rows.next()? {
358:                 Some(row) => {
359:                     let stored: String = row.get(0)?;
360:                     let is_secret: bool = row.get(1)?;
361:                     // Decrypt if secret
362:                     let val = if is_secret {
363:                         if let Some(sk) = &self.secret_keeper {
364:                             sk.decrypt(&stored).unwrap_or(stored)
365:                         } else {
366:                             stored
367:                         }
368:                     } else {
369:                         stored
370:                     };
371:                     Ok(Some(val))
372:                 }
373:                 None => Ok(None),
374:             }
375:         })
376:     }

382:             )?;

385:                     "path": row.get::<_, String>(0)?,

387:                     "is_secret": row.get::<_, bool>(2)?,
388:                     "created_at": row.get::<_, String>(3)?,
389:                 }))
390:             })?;
391:             let mut result = vec![];
392:             for row in rows {
393:                 result.push(row?);
394:             }
395:             Ok(result)
396:         })
397:     }

401:     pub fn set_resource(
402:         &self,
403:         path: &str,
404:         resource_type: &str,
405:         value: &serde_json::Value,
406:     ) -> Result<()> {
407:         let p = path.to_string();

410:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
411:             db.execute(
412:                 "INSERT INTO resources (path, resource_type, value) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3) ON CONFLICT(path) DO UPDATE SET resource_type=excluded.resource_type, value=excluded.value",
413:                 rusqlite::params![p, rt, v],
414:             )?;
415:             Ok(())
416:         })
417:     }

419:     pub fn get_resource(&self, path: &str) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>> {
420:         let p = path.to_string();
421:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
422:             let mut stmt =
423:                 db.prepare("SELECT resource_type, value FROM resources WHERE path = ?1")?;
424:             let mut rows = stmt.query(rusqlite::params![p])?;
425:             match rows.next()? {
426:                 Some(row) => {
427:                     let val_str: String = row.get(1)?;
428:                     Ok(Some(serde_json::json!({
429:                         "resource_type": row.get::<_, String>(0)?,
430:                         "value": serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&val_str).unwrap_or_default(),
431:                     })))
432:                 }
433:                 None => Ok(None),
434:             }
435:         })
436:     }

438:     pub fn list_resources(&self, resource_type: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>> {
439:         let rt = resource_type.map(|s| s.to_string());
440:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
441:             let rows = if let Some(ref t) = rt {
442:                 let mut stmt = db.prepare("SELECT path, resource_type FROM resources WHERE resource_type = ?1 ORDER BY path")?;
443:                 let rows = stmt.query_map(rusqlite::params![t], |row| {
444:                     Ok(serde_json::json!({"path": row.get::<_, String>(0)?, "type": row.get::<_, String>(1)?}))
445:                 })?;
446:                 let mut r = vec![];
447:                 for row in rows {
448:                     r.push(row?);
449:                 }
450:                 r
451:             } else {
452:                 let mut stmt =
453:                     db.prepare("SELECT path, resource_type FROM resources ORDER BY path")?;
454:                 let rows = stmt.query_map([], |row| {
455:                     Ok(serde_json::json!({"path": row.get::<_, String>(0)?, "type": row.get::<_, String>(1)?}))
456:                 })?;
457:                 let mut r = vec![];
458:                 for row in rows {
459:                     r.push(row?);
460:                 }
461:                 r
462:             };
463:             Ok(rows)
464:         })
465:     }

467:     /// Resolve `$var:path` and `$res:path` references in a JSON value.
468:     /// Recursively walks the value replacing `$var:X` with variable X's value
469:     /// and `$res:X` with resource X's JSON value.
470:     pub fn resolve_references(&self, val: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
471:         match val {
472:             serde_json::Value::String(s) => {
473:                 if let Some(var_path) = s.strip_prefix("$var:") {
474:                     match self.get_variable(var_path)? {
475:                         Some(v) => Ok(serde_json::Value::String(v)),
476:                         None => Ok(val.clone()),
477:                     }
478:                 } else if let Some(res_path) = s.strip_prefix("$res:") {
479:                     match self.get_resource(res_path)? {
480:                         Some(v) => Ok(v),
481:                         None => Ok(val.clone()),
482:                     }
483:                 } else {
484:                     Ok(val.clone())
485:                 }
486:             }
487:             serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {
488:                 let mut resolved = serde_json::Map::new();
489:                 for (k, v) in map {
490:                     resolved.insert(k.clone(), self.resolve_references(v)?);
491:                 }
492:                 Ok(serde_json::Value::Object(resolved))
493:             }
494:             serde_json::Value::Array(arr) => {
495:                 let resolved: Result<Vec<_>> = arr.iter().map(|v| self.resolve_references(v)).collect();
496:                 Ok(serde_json::Value::Array(resolved?))
497:             }
498:             other => Ok(other.clone()),
499:         }
500:     }

504:     pub fn enqueue(&self, kind: &str, target: &str, args: &serde_json::Value) -> Result<i64> {
505:         let k = kind.to_string();
506:         let t = target.to_string();
507:         let a = serde_json::to_string(args).map_err(|e| AutomatonError::Other(e.to_string()))?;
508:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
509:             db.execute(
510:                 "INSERT INTO jobs (kind, target_path, args) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)",
511:                 rusqlite::params![k, t, a],
512:             )?;
513:             Ok(db.last_insert_rowid())
514:         })
515:     }

520:                 "SELECT id, kind, target_path, args, scheduled_for, priority, running, worker_id, attempt FROM jobs ORDER BY priority DESC, scheduled_for ASC LIMIT ?1",
521:             )?;
522:             let rows = stmt.query_map(rusqlite::params![limit], |row| {
523:                 Ok(serde_json::json!({
524:                     "id": row.get::<_, i64>(0)?,
525:                     "kind": row.get::<_, String>(1)?,
526:                     "target_path": row.get::<_, String>(2)?,
527:                     "scheduled_for": row.get::<_, String>(4)?,
528:                     "priority": row.get::<_, i32>(5)?,
529:                     "running": row.get::<_, bool>(6)?,
530:                     "attempt": row.get::<_, i32>(8)?,
531:                 }))
532:             })?;
533:             let mut result = vec![];
534:             for row in rows {
535:                 result.push(row?);
536:             }
537:             Ok(result)
538:         })
539:     }

541:     /// Dequeue the highest-priority pending job (sets running=true, assigns worker_id)
542:     pub fn dequeue(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<Option<serde_json::Value>> {
543:         let wid = worker_id.to_string();
544:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
545:             // SQLite: simple LIMIT 1 approach (no SKIP LOCKED in SQLite)
546:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(
547:                 "SELECT id, kind, target_path, args FROM jobs WHERE NOT running AND scheduled_for <= datetime('now') ORDER BY priority DESC, scheduled_for ASC LIMIT 1"
548:             )?;
549:             let mut rows = stmt.query([])?;
550:             if let Some(row) = rows.next()? {
551:                 let id: i64 = row.get(0)?;
552:                 let kind: String = row.get(1)?;
553:                 let target: String = row.get(2)?;
554:                 let args_str: String = row.get(3)?;
555:                 // Mark as running (with race condition guard)
556:                 db.execute(
557:                     "UPDATE jobs SET running = 1, worker_id = ?1, attempt = attempt + 1 WHERE id = ?2 AND NOT running",
558:                     rusqlite::params![wid, id],
559:                 )?;
560:                 if db.changes() > 0 {
561:                     Ok(Some(serde_json::json!({
562:                         "id": id,
563:                         "kind": kind,
564:                         "target_path": target,
565:                         "args": serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&args_str).unwrap_or_default(),
566:                     })))
567:                 } else {
568:                     Ok(None) // Race condition: another worker claimed it
569:                 }
570:             } else {
571:                 Ok(None)
572:             }
573:         })
574:     }

576:     /// Mark a job as completed and remove it from the queue
577:     pub fn complete_job(&self, job_id: i64) -> Result<()> {
578:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
579:             db.execute("DELETE FROM jobs WHERE id = ?1", rusqlite::params![job_id])?;
580:             Ok(())
581:         })
582:     }

598:         let d = serde_json::to_string(definition).map_err(|e| AutomatonError::Other(e.to_string()))?;
599:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
600:             db.execute(
601:                 "INSERT INTO flows (id, path, version, definition, summary, on_failure) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, ?6)
602:                  ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE SET definition=excluded.definition, version=excluded.version",
603:                 rusqlite::params![i, p, v, d, summary, on_failure],
604:             )?;
605:             Ok(id)
606:         })
607:     }

614:             )?;

616:             match rows.next()? {

620:                         serde_json::from_str(&def_str).unwrap_or_default();
621:                     Ok(Some(serde_json::json!({
622:                         "id": row.get::<_, String>(0)?,
623:                         "path": row.get::<_, String>(1)?,
624:                         "version": row.get::<_, String>(2)?,
625:                         "definition": definition,
626:                         "summary": row.get::<_, Option<String>>(4)?,
627:                         "on_failure": row.get::<_, Option<String>>(5)?,
628:                         "created_at": row.get::<_, String>(6)?,
629:                     })))
630:                 }
631:                 None => Ok(None),
632:             }

635: 
636:     pub fn list_flows(&self) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>> {
637:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
638:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(
639:                 "SELECT id, path, version, created_at FROM flows ORDER BY path",
640:             )?;
641:             let rows = stmt.query_map([], |row| {
642:                 Ok(serde_json::json!({
643:                     "id": row.get::<_, String>(0)?,
644:                     "path": row.get::<_, String>(1)?,
645:                     "version": row.get::<_, String>(2)?,
646:                     "created_at": row.get::<_, String>(3)?,
647:                 }))
648:             })?;
649:             let mut result = vec![];
650:             for row in rows {
651:                 result.push(row?);
652:             }
653:             Ok(result)
654:         })
655:     }
656: 
657:     pub fn delete_flow(&self, path: &str) -> Result<()> {
658:         let p = path.to_string();
659:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
660:             db.execute("DELETE FROM flows WHERE path = ?1", rusqlite::params![p])?;
661:             Ok(())
662:         })
663:     }
664: 
665:     // ── Trigger operations ──
666: 
667:     pub fn create_trigger(
668:         &self,
669:         target: &str,
670:         is_flow: bool,
671:         ttype: &str,
672:         config: &serde_json::Value,
673:     ) -> Result<String> {
674:         let id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
675:         let i = id.clone();
676:         let t = target.to_string();
677:         let tp = ttype.to_string();
678:         let c = serde_json::to_string(config).map_err(|e| AutomatonError::Other(e.to_string()))?;
679:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
680:             db.execute(
681:                 "INSERT INTO triggers (id, target_path, target_is_flow, trigger_type, config) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5)",
682:                 rusqlite::params![i, t, is_flow as i32, tp, c],
683:             )?;
684:             Ok(id)
685:         })
686:     }
687: 
688:     pub fn get_enabled_triggers(&self, ttype: &str) -> Result<Vec<serde_json::Value>> {
689:         let tp = ttype.to_string();
690:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
691:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(
692:                 "SELECT id, target_path, target_is_flow, config, created_at FROM triggers WHERE enabled AND trigger_type = ?1",
693:             )?;
694:             let rows = stmt.query_map(rusqlite::params![tp], |row| {
695:                 let config_str: String = row.get(3)?;
696:                 Ok(serde_json::json!({
697:                     "id": row.get::<_, String>(0)?,
698:                     "target_path": row.get::<_, String>(1)?,
699:                     "target_is_flow": row.get::<_, bool>(2)?,
700:                     "config": serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(&config_str).unwrap_or_default(),
701:                     "created_at": row.get::<_, String>(4)?,
702:                 }))
703:             })?;
704:             let mut result = vec![];
705:             for row in rows {
706:                 result.push(row?);
707:             }
708:             Ok(result)
709:         })
710:     }
711: }

use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};

use crate::module::{ContentHash, DepRef, RetryConfig};

/// The serializable manifest for an automation module
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]
pub struct AutomationManifest {
    pub name: String,
    pub version: String,
    pub entry: String,
    pub summary: Option<String>,
    pub description: Option<String>,
    pub inputs_schema: SchemaMode,
    pub outputs_schema: SchemaMode,
    pub permissions: Vec<String>,
    pub resources: Vec<String>,
    pub depends_on: Vec<DepRef>,
    pub retry: Option<RetryConfig>,
    pub timeout_ms: u64,
    pub tags: Vec<String>,

input.thought:
Complete codebase analysis complete. I've read 14+ crates, all core types, both execution engines, all 32 MCP tools, graph store, registry (SQLite+Postgres), REST API, CLI, and all 28 integration tests. Now constructing the plan. Key architectural findings:
- Two execution engines: Engine (DAG) and FlowEngine (step-based)
- 25 param structs, zero with deny_unknown_fields
- Graph has no property indexes, all filtering is in-memory
- no streaming/progress capability at all
- Postgres schema exists but no migration tool from SQLite
- No rate limiting, webhooks, plugin system
- created_at already exists on nodes/edges

The plan is organized by dependency order, not by priority. This ensures each group can be built/tested independently.
